items.title: health poller marks instance active on SSH timeout fallback even when agent never started
items.intent: An instance must only be marked active when the agent is actually reachable. The SSH port timeout fallback that returns healthy:true after 18 attempts is incorrect — SSH up does not mean agent up.
items.description: In src/cloud/provisioning/health.ts lines 86-93, after 18 poll attempts (~3 minutes) where SSH is open but the dashboard port (3737) is not, pollInstanceHealth() returns healthy:true with the comment 'cloud-init may still be running but VM is functional'. This is wrong: SSH being up means the VM booted, not that the agent is running. With the clawhq up failure masking bug (BUG-109), this fallback causes the provisioning engine to mark instances active even when the agent never started. Even with BUG-109 fixed, the fallback allows a VM with a failed cloud-init to be reported as healthy. Fix: change the fallback to return healthy:false with a clear error — if port 3737 is not open after 3 minutes, the agent has not started.
steps: Read src/cloud/provisioning/health.ts lines 52-105 to understand the current polling logic.

At lines 86-93, change the fallback: instead of returning healthy:true, return healthy:false with error 'Agent did not become reachable on port 3737 within the expected window. SSH is accessible — connect with clawhq deploy update or check cloud-init logs: journalctl -u cloud-init'.

Verify that the tests in health.test.ts (if they exist) cover the timeout path — if not, add a test for: SSH open + dashboard never opens + 19+ poll attempts = healthy:false with expected error message.
Run npm test -- --testPathPattern=health to confirm all tests pass.
acceptanceCriteria: pollInstanceHealth() returns healthy:false when SSH is reachable but port 3737 never opens, regardless of number of attempts
The error message in the result includes the SSH access hint for debugging
Existing health tests pass with no regression
items.groomingNotes: SPRINT AFTER BUG-109. The health poller's SSH-open fallback returns healthy:true after 18 attempts — this conflates 'VM booted' with 'agent running'. Even with BUG-109 fixed (failed cloud-init now exits non-zero), a VM whose cloud-init silently misbehaves in other ways could still trigger this path. The fix is conceptually simple: healthy:false on timeout with a clear message and SSH debug hint. FIX SCOPE: health.ts lines 86-93 + one new test case for the timeout path in health.test.ts. AC is correct and user-facing. Note: the error message wording in AC should match step 2 exactly — sprinter should use the journalctl hint verbatim.

items.title: credentials.ts and snapshot-registry.ts create cloud/ dir without DIR_MODE_SECRET — inconsistent with registry.ts
items.intent: Every file and directory under ~/.clawhq/cloud/ must be created with secure permissions. The cloud/ directory must never be world-readable — it contains credentials.json, instances.json, and snapshots.json. Permissions must be consistent across all writers.
items.description: src/cloud/provisioning/credentials.ts:53 calls mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) without mode. src/cloud/provisioning/snapshot-registry.ts:51 does the same. In contrast, src/cloud/provisioning/registry.ts:57 correctly passes { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET }. If credentials.ts or snapshot-registry.ts runs first (e.g. user runs 'clawhq cloud connect' before first provision), it creates ~/.clawhq/cloud/ with the default umask (0755 — world-readable). Later, registry.ts cannot fix the mode because mkdirSync with recursive:true does not re-chmod existing directories. Result: other local users can list ~/.clawhq/cloud/ and enumerate its files. Additionally, snapshot-registry.ts:59 writes snapshots.json via writeFileSync(tmpPath, content) with no mode — the file will be world-readable. credentials.ts correctly uses FILE_MODE_SECRET on write. Fix: add DIR_MODE_SECRET to both mkdirSync calls; add FILE_MODE_SECRET to snapshot-registry.ts writeFileSync call.
steps: Read src/cloud/provisioning/credentials.ts:50-54 and src/cloud/provisioning/snapshot-registry.ts:48-52 to confirm mkdirSync calls lack mode. Read src/cloud/provisioning/registry.ts:55-58 to confirm DIR_MODE_SECRET usage as the reference pattern.

In src/cloud/provisioning/credentials.ts, import DIR_MODE_SECRET from '../../config/defaults.js' (already imported in registry.ts — verify the export exists). Change mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET }).

In src/cloud/provisioning/snapshot-registry.ts, import DIR_MODE_SECRET and FILE_MODE_SECRET from '../../config/defaults.js'. Change mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET }). Change writeFileSync(tmpPath, content) to writeFileSync(tmpPath, content, { mode: FILE_MODE_SECRET }).

Verify that registry.test.ts already covers the FILE_MODE_SECRET and DIR_MODE_SECRET assertions (it does per existing tests). Add equivalent assertions to any snapshot-registry test or credentials test if they exist — if not, note the gap in grooming notes.
Run npm test -- --testPathPattern='registry|credentials|snapshot' to confirm no regression.
acceptanceCriteria: credentials.ts creates ~/.clawhq/cloud/ with DIR_MODE_SECRET when the directory does not exist
snapshot-registry.ts creates ~/.clawhq/cloud/ with DIR_MODE_SECRET when the directory does not exist
All existing registry, credentials, and provisioning tests pass with no regression
items.groomingNotes: PRIORITY ELEVATED 2026-03-24: Elevated from 'should' to 'must'. Security non-negotiable: 'Security by default — hardening is automatic, not opt-in.' A world-readable ~/.clawhq/cloud/ directory containing credentials.json and instances.json violates this non-negotiable for any multi-user system. The race condition (credentials.ts runs before registry.ts, sets permissive mode that cannot be retroactively fixed) means current installs may already be exposed. 3-line fix. Sprint immediately.
